perturbation.original_text: (vi) "Program Content", with respect to this Agreement, shall mean all program content set forth in Exhibit 1 to which Party A owns Internet and media copyrights and which are required for Party B Business, including but not limited to programs on news, policy trends, entertainment, business and economic trends.
perturbation.changed_text: (vi) "Program Content", with respect to this Agreement, shall mean program content to which Party A owns Internet and media copyrights and which are required for Party B Business, including programs on news, policy trends, entertainment, business and economic trends.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'all program content set forth in Exhibit 1' from the definition of 'Program Content' creates ambiguity. Exhibit 1 is later referenced as defining the specific program content licensed. Without this explicit inclusion in the definition, there's uncertainty about whether all content in Exhibit 1 is automatically included. This contradicts the later section that relies on Exhibit 1 to define the scope of licensed content.
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE THREE SERVICE SCOPE AND METHOD OF PROVISION 3.2
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Program Content to be licensed by Party A to Party B shall be as set forth in Exhibit 1 hereto, as updated from time to time. If the Program Content required by Party B is beyond that listed on Exhibit 1, as updated from time to time, Party B shall send its written request to Party A promptly and the latter shall license the Program Content described in the preceding phrase to Party B to the extent it has power to do so in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'all program content set forth in Exhibit 1' from the definition of 'Program Content' introduces ambiguity regarding the scope of licensed content. The contradicted text explicitly states that 'The Program Content to be licensed by Party A to Party B shall be as set forth in Exhibit 1 hereto'. Without the explicit inclusion in the definition of 'Program Content', there's uncertainty about whether all content in Exhibit 1 is automatically included in the definition of Program Content. This creates a contradiction because the definition of Program Content becomes unclear, while the second section still relies on Exhibit 1 to define the licensed content. Thus, the Changed Text creates a conflict in that it is unclear if Exhibit 1 is a definitive list of the Program Content to be licensed, as the Contradicted Text states.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 The amount of the service fee and its terms of payment shall be as set forth in Attachment 1 to the "Agreement Between Phoenix Satellite TV and Phoenix New Media Regarding Cooperation in the Fields of Content, Branding, Promotion and Technology" dated November 24, 2009 between Phoenix Satellite Television Holdings Limited and Phoenix Online (Beijing) Information Technology Co., Ltd. ("Phoenix Online").
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 The amount of the service fee shall be negotiated between both parties and adhere to market rates. Terms of payment will be agreed upon, contingent on project specifics.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing the reference to 'Attachment 1' with a vague statement about 'negotiated' fees and 'project specifics' eliminates the defined payment terms. This is an omission because the payment terms are critical to the contract. It creates an in-text contradiction with section 4.2, which assumes the service fee is already established.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties may enter into a separate agreement and establish specific fee rates in respect of services beyond this Agreement in accordance with the principles set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the service fee is determined by Attachment 1, providing a clear method for fee calculation. The changed text replaces this with a negotiation process based on market rates and project specifics. This creates a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text' which assumes an existing fee structure based on the agreement and allows for separate agreements for services 'beyond this Agreement'. The changed text makes it unclear how the 'principles set forth herein' can be applied to services not defined by Attachment 1 because it's been replaced with a negotiation, effectively removing the initial framework for establishing fees which the Contradicted Text relies on. This aligns with the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition because essential language specifying the fee structure has been removed, creating a contradiction with the Contradicted Text which assumes the fee structure is already defined.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that a Party's performance of this Agreement or any covenants of the Parties is directly affected by an earthquake, typhoon, flood, fire, war, computer virus, design loophole in any software tool, hacker attack on the Internet, amendment to law or policy or any other event of force majeure which is not foreseeable or the result of which is not to be prevented or avoided, such Party shall immediately give the other Party a notice by fax of such event and within thirty days (30) thereafter provide a detailed report thereof as well as a certification document explaining the cause for the non-performance or delayed performance of this Agreement, which certification document shall be issued by the public notary of the region in which the event of force majeure occurred. The Parties shall decide through consultation whether performance of this Agreement, in whole or in part, shall be relieved or delayed to the extent affected by such event. With respect to economic losses sustained by either Party as a result of such event, neither Party shall be liable therefor.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that a Party's performance of this Agreement or any covenants of the Parties is directly affected by an earthquake, typhoon, flood, or fire, such Party shall immediately give the other Party a notice by fax of such event. The Parties shall decide through consultation whether performance of this Agreement, in whole or in part, shall be relieved or delayed to the extent affected by such event. With respect to economic losses sustained by either Party as a result of such event, neither Party shall be liable therefor.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'war, computer virus, design loophole in any software tool, hacker attack on the Internet, amendment to law or policy or any other event of force majeure which is not foreseeable or the result of which is not to be prevented or avoided' from force majeure definition, the text contradicts 8.2.3. This is because 8.2.3 references the prc law where the term force majeure is not restricted to just those terms as defined in the omission. It now implies a lack of coverage for events impacting performance
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE EIGHT LIABILITIES FOR BREACH; TERMINATION 8.2.3
perturbation.contradicted_text: one Party's performance of its obligations hereunder is held unlawful under the PRC Law, such Party may send a written notice of termination to the other Party upon the promulgation of the relevant PRC Law;
perturbation.justification: The omission of specific events like 'war, computer virus, design loophole in any software tool, hacker attack on the Internet, amendment to law or policy or any other event of force majeure which is not foreseeable or the result of which is not to be prevented or avoided' from the definition of force majeure in the changed text creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text. The contradicted text refers to 'PRC Law' under which a party's obligations are deemed unlawful. By narrowing the definition of force majeure, the modified text implies that events outside the revised definition (e.g., a change in law making performance unlawful under PRC Law) are *not* covered under force majeure, thereby contradicting the assumption in the original contract and the reference to PRC Law in the contradicted text. This change creates a significant conflict regarding what constitutes an excusable event.
